---
status: current
last-trued: 2026-06-21
trued-against: HEAD@main on 2026-06-21
---

## Changelog

- _2026-06-21_ — initial draft + front-matter, on the day created. As-built audit of every `NavigationSplitView` detail pane. Verbatim string citations (`status_page.py`, `en/desktop.json`, `UnsupportedSubsetView.swift`, `PipelineRunner.swift`) verified exact. Caveat: the `ContentView.swift` gallery `file:line` refs are **approximate (~8–13 lines off)** — written against a ContentView a few commits before the `warm-sidecar-pool` merge settled the line numbers; each lands in the right function/region but a few lines above the actual `case` branch. Re-anchor on the next pass. For the run-progress subtitle, `ProjectSubtitle.swift` (`resolve` precedence) is canonical — see `design-desktop-project-status.md` §4.

# Detail-pane catalogue — macOS desktop shell

> **What this is.** An audit of every distinct "detail pane" (the main content area
> of the `NavigationSplitView`) that the macOS app can show — across selection
> (none / one / many), project lifecycle, availability, and server state. The goal
> is to see the whole set at once so we can design these panes as a *coherent
> system* rather than one-at-a-time.
>
> **The images are synthetic mockups, not live captures.** The real SwiftUI panes
> need the signed sidecar + App Sandbox stack, which can't run headless. Each frame
> is drawn to macOS proportions from the *actual* code paths and copy, including the
> sidebar state, because half the story is that **the sidebar carries state the
> detail pane does not**. Source of truth for behaviour is always the code
> (`file:line` refs below); these mockups are a design aid.
>
> Mockups live in [`mockups/detail-panes/`](mockups/detail-panes/) — `svg/` (vector
> source, Figma-importable), `png/` (rasterised screenshots). The images below are
> **embedded as SVG data URIs** so this doc renders self-contained in any preview
> (iA Writer, the Claude desktop preview, etc.) without needing filesystem access to
> the image folder. Regenerate the mockups with `mockups/detail-panes/render-png.sh`,
> then re-embed with `python3 mockups/detail-panes/embed-images.py`. Generated 21 Jun 2026.

## ⚠️ Mockups, not screenshots — but the text is real

Every image here is a **synthetic mockup drawn by Claude**, not a screen capture of the
running macOS app. **Layout, icons, type, and colour are placeholders** — the icons
especially are crude stand-ins to be redesigned.

**The wording, however, now matches the real shipped strings** for every pane that
exists in code (pulled from `bristlenose/locales/en/desktop.json` and the server
literals), so you *can* read the current copy off the images. The only text that's
illustrative is **dynamic runtime data** — project names, the volume name, counts, ETAs,
and error/cause messages (e.g. N08's body, W04's cause) — plus the **red GAP
annotations**, which are my commentary, not UI. Where a string is shown verbatim it
carries the app's quirks intact (Title-Case headlines, the iCloud pane reusing the drive
hint, "items" vs "projects"). The [**Exact current strings**](#exact-current-strings-verbatim-audit)
table below is the authoritative, keyed reference for the end-to-end rewrite.

## How to read these

The pane you see is the result of **two decision trees in series**:

1. **Native gate** — [`ContentView.detail`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1824)
   branches on *selection cardinality → availability → path/shape → serve-process
   state*. It does **not** inspect `PipelineState` (running / failed / partial …)
   except one helper that gates the subset view.
2. **Web gate** — once the native gate lands on "serve is running," it hands off to a
   `WKWebView`, and the **server** then decides via
   [`detect_status()`](../bristlenose/server/status_page.py:109): show a status page
   (no-run / cancelled / failed / build-broken) or fall through to the React SPA.

So "what's in the pane during a run" isn't owned by either layer cleanly — it falls
between them. That's where the accidental panes live.

Each frame is tagged with a verdict:

| Tag | Meaning |
|-----|---------|
| 🟢 **Designed** | Intentional surface with real copy, icon, a11y. Keep. |
| 🔵 **Exists (product)** | The React report itself — already designed, out of scope for this exercise. |
| ⚪ **Thin** | Functional but generic / under-designed; created to fill the gate. |
| 🟠 **Accidental** | Plumbing byproduct — shown because *something* had to be there. |
| 🔴 **Gap** | No pane exists; the area shows whatever the two gates happen to produce. |

Scope: the **detail column only**. Settings (separate `Settings` scene), the
AI-consent sheet, the diagnostic popover, toasts, and the icon picker are
overlays/scenes, not detail panes — referenced only where they substitute for a
missing pane.

---

## Gallery

### A · StatePane family (native gate)

These are all the same molecule — a centred `{icon, headline, body, 0–1 action}`.
Seven of them are literally `ContentUnavailableView`.

#### N01 · Unavailable — unmounted volume — 🟢
[`ContentView.swift:1909`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1909)
![N01-unavailable-volume](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Drive ejected. Names the volume; **no in-app action** (mount it externally). Sidebar row dims + trailing drive glyph.

#### N02 · Unavailable — moved / missing — 🟢
[`ContentView.swift:1917`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1917)
![N02-unavailable-moved](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Folder renamed/deleted. Offers the one fix that exists: **Locate…** (Spotlight + picker).

#### N03 · Unavailable — in iCloud — 🟢
[`ContentView.swift:1929`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1929)
![N03-unavailable-cloud](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Evicted/syncing (not broken) — distinct precedence from unmount. Waits, no action.

#### N04 · Empty project — drag interviews — 🟢
[`ContentView.swift:1831`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1831)
![N04-empty-path](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

New project, no files yet. The drop target is the **sidebar**, not this pane — the affordance is off-pane.

#### N05 · File-subset project (can't analyse) — ⚪
[`UnsupportedSubsetView.swift`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/UnsupportedSubsetView.swift)
![N05-subset-files](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Made from individual files; CLI can't analyse the shape yet. Honest, but doesn't say "make a folder instead." Placeholder for an unshipped feature.

#### N10 · Multiple projects selected — ⚪
[`ContentView.swift:1881`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1881)
![N10-multi-select](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Multi-select is supported in the sidebar but the pane gates it. Generic.

#### N12 · No selection (projects exist) — ⚪
[`ContentView.swift:1888`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1888)
![N12-no-selection](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Also the **single-folder-selected fallthrough** — selecting a folder silently lands here (folders have no report).

### B · Launcher

#### N11 · Welcome / first run (zero projects) — 🟢
[`WelcomeView.swift`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/WelcomeView.swift)
![N11-welcome-firstrun](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Deliberately designed launcher (replaced an old stub): app icon, tagline, New Project + Drop cards, AI & Privacy link. Sidebar empty.

### C · Boot family (native, around the webview)

#### N06 · Starting sidecar — 🟢
[`ContentView.swift:1852`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1852)
![N06-boot-starting](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Spinner + "Starting…" while the analysis server boots.

#### N07 · Loading report — ⚪
[`ContentView.swift:1865`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1865)
![N07-boot-loading-report](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Overlay over the webview. Clears on a **blind 2s timeout** because status pages never post `ready` ([desktop gotcha](../desktop/CLAUDE.md)) — a timing hack, not a designed handoff.

#### N08 · Server failed to start — 🟢
[`ContentView.swift:1873`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1873)
![N08-serve-failed](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The *server* failed (distinct from a *run* failing). Retry + Show details.

### D · Report (web — the product, already designed)

#### N09 · Report — Project / Dashboard tab — 🔵
[`ContentView.swift:1856`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1856) → React SPA
![N09-report-dashboard](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

#### W10 · Report — Quotes tab — 🔵
![W10-report-quotes](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The five SPA tabs (Project, Sessions+Transcript, Quotes, Codebook, Analysis) each have their own loading / error / empty sub-states. Existing, designed — shown here only for completeness.

### E · Web status pages (inside the running webview)

#### W01 · No run yet (desktop) — 🟢
[`status_page.py:131`](../bristlenose/server/status_page.py:131)
![W01-status-no-run-desktop](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Legit for "selected, nothing analysed." But see **GAP-analysing** — the same page is shown *during* a first run.

#### W02 · No run yet (CLI build) — 🟢
[`status_page.py:138`](../bristlenose/server/status_page.py:138)
![W02-status-no-run-cli](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

CLI-only copy (`$ bristlenose run …`); desktop never sees it.

#### W03 · Last run cancelled — 🟢
[`status_page.py:158`](../bristlenose/server/status_page.py:158)
![W03-status-cancelled](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Web-rendered cancel + cause/log disclosure. **Duplicates** the native popover surface.

#### W04 · Last run failed — 🟢
[`status_page.py:166`](../bristlenose/server/status_page.py:166)
![W04-status-failed](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Same failure the native diagnostic popover also renders — different layer, different copy.

#### W05 · Build incomplete (fail-loud 500) — 🟢
[`app.py:625`](../bristlenose/server/app.py:625)
![W05-build-incomplete](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

React bundle missing. Refuses to fall back to static render (guards BUG-3 class).

#### W06 · Unhandled 500 — ⚪
[`app.py:108`](../bristlenose/server/app.py:108)
![W06-server-500](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Generic error stub.

### F · Gaps (no pane — the design surface)

#### GAP-analysing · Run in progress — 🔴
![GAP-analysing](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

**The big one.** There is no "analysing" detail pane. The sidebar shows "Analysing · 2 of 6 · ETA"; the main area shows the no-run status page ("drop a folder here"). All run progress lives in the row. Flagged as an *open product requirement* in [`design-desktop-project-status.md`](design-desktop-project-status.md).

#### GAP-failed-duplication · Failure in two surfaces — 🔴
![GAP-failed-duplication](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Run failure renders as **both** a native diagnostic popover (sidebar-anchored) **and** a web status page (the pane) — two vocabularies for one state.

#### GAP-queued-stopped-partial · No pane for terminal-ish states — 🔴
![GAP-queued-stopped-partial](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

`queued` → stale report · `stopped` → stale report · `partial` → half report + popover. No "resume", "continue to analysis", or "partial — N sessions failed" surface in the pane.

---

## Catalogue — native gate

`Δ actions` = what the user *can* vs *can't* do from that pane.

| ID | State / condition | What's shown now | Δ actions | Verdict |
|----|------|------|------|------|
| N01 | `cantFind(.unmountedVolume/.networkUnreachable)` | drive glyph + volume name + hint | **none** | 🟢 |
| N02 | `cantFind(.moved/.missingBookmark)` | folder-? + "moved" + **Locate…** | re-point folder | 🟢 |
| N03 | `inCloud` | cloud + "downloading from iCloud" | none (waits) | 🟢 |
| N04 | `path.isEmpty` | tray + "Drag interviews here" | none in-pane (drop on sidebar) | 🟢 |
| N05 | `inputFiles != nil` & no viewable data | "analyses folders" + file list | reveal files; **can't analyse** | ⚪ |
| N06 | serve `.idle/.starting` | spinner + "Starting…" | none | 🟢 |
| N07 | serve `.running` & `!isReady` | spinner overlay, 2s-timeout clear | none | ⚪ |
| N08 | serve `.failed` | warning + **Retry** + details | retry serve | 🟢 |
| N09 | serve `.running` & ready | → React SPA (web gate) | full report tooling | 🔵 |
| N10 | `selectionCount > 1` | stack + "N selected / pick one" | none | ⚪ |
| N11 | no selection, **zero** projects | launcher | new / drop / consent | 🟢 |
| N12 | no selection (or 1 folder) | "Pick a project" + New Project | new project | ⚪ |

## Catalogue — web gate (inside N09)

| ID | State / condition | What's shown now | Δ actions | Verdict |
|----|------|------|------|------|
| W01 | desktop, `entry is None` | "No interviews to analyse yet / drop a folder" | none | 🟢 (🟠 in run case) |
| W02 | CLI, `entry is None` | "Nothing to see here / `$ bristlenose run`" | none | 🟢 |
| W03 | last run `CANCELLED` | "Last run was cancelled" + cause + log | feedback/help links | 🟢 |
| W04 | last run `FAILED` | "Last run failed" + cause + log | feedback/help links | 🟢 |
| W05 | React bundle missing | "Build incomplete" 500 | none | 🟢 |
| W06 | unhandled exception | "Internal Server Error" | none | ⚪ |
| W07–W12 | last run `COMPLETED` | SPA tabs (each w/ loading/error/empty) | full tooling | 🔵 |

## Catalogue — lifecycle states with no pane

These `PipelineState` cases ([`PipelineRunner.swift:81`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/PipelineRunner.swift:81)) are
fully modelled and shown in the **sidebar row**, but the detail pane has nothing for
them — it shows whatever the two gates produce.

| Pipeline state | What the pane actually shows | Where the user learns the real state |
|------|------|------|
| `queued` | stale report / W01 | sidebar subtitle |
| `running` (first run) | **W01 "drop a folder"** | sidebar ring + "Analysing · ETA" |
| `running` (re-run) | the **previous** report | sidebar ring |
| `stopped` | stale report; W03 after terminus | sidebar + popover |
| `partial` / `completedPartial` | stale/half report; native popover | popover; pane silent |
| `failed` / `failedWithDiagnostic` | W04 **and/or** native popover | both, divergent wording |

The auto-reload that swaps a status page for the finished report is gated on the
`analysing → ready` transition only — so `failed`/`partial` transitions don't
reliably refresh the pane; it can sit on stale content until a manual reload or
project re-select.

---

## Exact current strings (verbatim audit)

The **ground truth for the rewrite** — the actual strings the app renders today, pulled
from `bristlenose/locales/en/desktop.json` (i18n keys, shown as `namespace.key`),
`bristlenose/server/status_page.py` (server literals), and Swift string literals where a
view isn't internationalised. `{{…}}` / `%d` / `%@` mark runtime-interpolated values.
**This — not the text on the mockups — is what users currently see.**

### Native panes (SwiftUI)

| ID | Element | Source key | Exact current string |
|----|---------|-----------|----------------------|
| N01 | Headline | `chrome.projectUnavailable` | Project Unavailable |
| N01 | Volume line | _dynamic_ `name` | e.g. `Samsung T7` |
| N01 | Hint | `chrome.projectUnavailableHint` | Connect the drive to access this project. |
| N01 | Sidebar subtitle | `availability.unmountedVolume` | `{{name}} · missing` |
| N01n | Sidebar (network) | `availability.networkUnreachable` | `{{host}} · unreachable` |
| N02 | Headline | `chrome.projectMoved` | Project Moved or Deleted |
| N02 | Body | `chrome.projectMovedDescription` | The project folder couldn't be found. It may have been moved or deleted. |
| N02 | Button | `chrome.locate` | Locate… |
| N03 | Headline | `availability.inCloud` | In iCloud |
| N03 | Body ⚠️ | `chrome.projectUnavailableHint` | Connect the drive to access this project. |
| N04 | Headline | `chrome.dragInterviews` | Drag Interviews Here |
| N04 | Body | `chrome.dragInterviewsDescription` | Add interview recordings or transcripts to get started. |
| N05 | Heading | `UnsupportedSubsetView.swift:20` _(literal, not i18n)_ | Bristlenose analyses folders |
| N05 | Body | `:26` _(literal)_ | This project was created from individual files, so it can't be analysed. |
| N05 | Section | `:32` _(literal)_ | Files in this project |
| N05 | Per-file action | `:77` _(literal)_ | Show in Finder |
| N06 | Headline | `boot.startingSidecar` | Starting Bristlenose… |
| N06 | Tagline (body) | `boot.tagline` | Sensemaking for User Research |
| N07 | Headline | `boot.loadingReport` | Loading report… |
| N07 | Tagline (body) | `boot.tagline` | Sensemaking for User Research |
| N08 | Headline | `boot.failedTitle` | Couldn't start Bristlenose |
| N08 | Body | _dynamic_ error | the captured error message |
| N08 | Buttons | `boot.retry` · `boot.showDetails` / `boot.hideDetails` | Retry · Show details / Hide details |
| N10 | Headline | `chrome.multipleSelected` | `%d items selected` |
| N10 | Body | `chrome.multipleSelectedHint` | Select a single project to view its report. |
| N11 | Title | `chrome.welcomeTitle` | Welcome to Bristlenose |
| N11 | Subtitle | `welcome.subtitle` | Turn a folder of interviews into a report you can share — on your laptop, nothing uploaded. |
| N11 | Card 1 | `welcome.newProject` · `welcome.newProjectHint` | New Project · Pick a folder of recordings or transcripts and start fresh. |
| N11 | Card 2 | `welcome.dropFolderTitle` · `welcome.dropFolderHint` | Drop a folder · Drag a folder of recordings or transcripts here to add it as a project. |
| N11 | Link | `welcome.aiPrivacyLink` | Review AI & privacy settings… |
| N11 | Step 1 | `welcome.steps.ingest*` | Add interviews — Audio, video, or existing transcripts. |
| N11 | Step 2 | `welcome.steps.process*` | Bristlenose analyses — Transcribe, find quotes, cluster themes. |
| N11 | Step 3 | `welcome.steps.export*` | Browse & export — Read findings, export HTML or CSV. |
| N12 | Headline | `chrome.noProjectSelected` | No Project Selected |
| N12 | Body | `chrome.selectProject` | Select a project from the sidebar. |
| N12 | Button | `welcome.newProject` | New Project |
| N12 | Empty-sidebar hint | `chrome.emptyStateHint` | Drag a folder here or press ⌘N |

### Server status pages (in the webview)

| ID | Element | Source | Exact current string |
|----|---------|--------|----------------------|
| W01 | Headline | `status_page.py:134` | No interviews to analyse yet. |
| W01 | Body | `:135` | Drop a folder of interviews here to start. |
| W02 | Headline | `:140` | Nothing to see here, yet. |
| W02 | Body (mono) | `:141` | `$ bristlenose run interviews/` |
| W03 | Headline | `:161` | Last run was cancelled. |
| W03 | Body | `:162` | Re-run when ready. |
| W03/W04 | Details disclosure | _dynamic_ | cause (category · stage · provider · message) + last 4 KB of `bristlenose.log` |
| W04 | Headline | `:170` | Last run failed. |
| W04 | Body | _dynamic_ `cause.message` | provider/stage-specific; absent if not captured |
| W05 | — | `app.py` (~625) | "Build incomplete" page — exact body not pinned here; verify in `app.py` before quoting |
| W06 | — | `app.py:108` | Internal Server Error |

### Run-progress (sidebar subtitle — drives GAP-analysing)

| Element | Source key | Exact current string |
|---------|-----------|----------------------|
| Base | `chrome.pipeline.analysing` | Analysing… |
| Stage labels | `chrome.pipeline.stage.*` | Transcribing · Identifying speakers · Finding topics · Finding quotes · Grouping themes · Building report |
| Session count | `chrome.pipeline.sessionsCount` | `{{complete}} of {{total}}` |
| ETA | `chrome.pipeline.etaUnderMinute` · `etaMinutes` | <1 min left · ~`{{count}}` min left |
| Queued | `chrome.pipeline.queuedPosition` | `Queued · position {{position}}` |
| Other terminal | `chrome.pipeline.*` | Stopping… · Transcribed · Partial run · Stopped |

### Inconsistencies this audit surfaced (rewrite candidates)

- **Mixed casing.** Native detail headlines are **Title Case** ("Project Unavailable",
  "Drag Interviews Here", "Project Moved or Deleted", "No Project Selected") while server
  status pages are **sentence case** ("No interviews to analyse yet."). Pick one.
- **iCloud reuses the drive hint.** `availability.inCloud`'s body is
  `chrome.projectUnavailableHint` = "Connect the drive to access this project." — wrong
  advice for a cloud-evicted file.
- **`UnsupportedSubsetView` (N05) is not internationalised** — hardcoded English literals,
  unlike every other pane. Any rewrite must also move it into the locale files.
- **Multi-select says "items"** (`%d items selected`) but the hint says "project", and
  projects are the only selectable thing — should be "projects".
- **Three near-duplicate "starting/loading" strings** across namespaces:
  `chrome.startingServer` "Starting server…", `boot.startingSidecar` "Starting
  Bristlenose…", `pipeline.status.starting` "Starting…". Consolidate.
- **W04 / N08 bodies are runtime values** (cause / error message) — the rewrite defines a
  *tone and template* for these, not a fixed string.

---

## Design synthesis

**Keep, already designed:** the report SPA (N09 / W07–12), the first-run launcher
(N11), the three boot phases (N06–N08), the unavailable family (N01–N03).

**One clearly accidental pane:** **W01 shown during an active run** (GAP-analysing).
The server status page predates any notion of in-flight runs, so an actively-
transcribing first run shows "drop a folder here" while the sidebar spins. Purest
"we needed *something* there" artifact.

**One structural gap, highest value:** there is **no "analysing" detail pane.** For a
tool whose loop is "drop → wait minutes → read report," the main area being
blank/misleading during the wait is the most valuable thing to design. Already an
open product requirement.

**Two layers render the same state, differently.** Failure and cancellation appear
as both a **native** diagnostic popover (sidebar vocabulary) and a **web** status
page (W3/W4). For a coherent set, pick **one owner per post-terminal state** —
native ownership is more consistent with the sidebar and lets the pane read the
terminus directly instead of waiting on a webview reload.

### The collapse map

Ten panes are the **same molecule** — centred `{icon, headline, body, 0–1 action}`:

> N01, N02, N03, N04, N05, N10, N12 (native) + W01, W03, W04 (web)

Design one component — call it **StatePane** — with a variation table (icon, copy,
optional single action, tone: waiting / empty / error). Instantiate it ~10×, don't
design ten panes. SwiftUI already hints at this (N01–N04/N10 are all
`ContentUnavailableView`).

That leaves only **three things that aren't the StatePane** and need bespoke design:

1. **The report SPA** — exists.
2. **The boot/progress family** (N06–N08) — and the *missing* analysing pane should
   **join this family**, not the StatePane family. The code comment at
   [`ContentView.swift:1862`](../desktop/Bristlenose/Bristlenose/ContentView.swift:1862)
   already reaches for it ("same icon + tagline … so the eye doesn't relocate"): a
   run-progress pane that shares the boot spine + the determinate ring/ETA the
   sidebar already computes is a small extension, not a new surface.
3. **The launcher** (N11) — exists.

So the real design surface reduces to: **one StatePane molecule (×10 variations),
one extension of the boot family into an analysing pane, and a decision to retire
W01/W03/W04 in favour of native ownership of post-terminal states.** Plus the
unbuilt **Get Info** pane (planned, not started) if/when project metadata is wanted.

---

## Provenance

- **Generator:** [`mockups/detail-panes/generate.py`](mockups/detail-panes/generate.py)
  (pure stdlib, no deps). Edit the `build()` catalogue, re-run, re-rasterise.
- **Rasteriser:** [`mockups/detail-panes/render-png.sh`](mockups/detail-panes/render-png.sh)
  (uses macOS `qlmanage` — square canvas avoids Quick Look's thumbnail crop).
- **Code paths audited:** `ContentView.detail` (native gate), `status_page.detect_status`
  (web gate), `PipelineRunner.PipelineState` (lifecycle), `WelcomeView` /
  `UnsupportedSubsetView` / `BootView`.
- **Related design docs:** [`design-desktop-project-status.md`](design-desktop-project-status.md)
  (state model + the "detail pane is an open requirement" note),
  [`design-project-sidebar.md`](design-project-sidebar.md),
  [`design-pipeline-diagnostic-popover.md`](design-pipeline-diagnostic-popover.md),
  [`design-desktop-app.md`](design-desktop-app.md).
